title: Static fields should not be updated in constructors
description: Static fields should not be updated in constructorsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?Assigning a value to astaticfield in a constructor could cause unreliable behavior at runtime since it will change the value for all instances of the class.Instead remove the field’sstaticmodifier, or initialize it statically.Noncompliant code examplepublic class Person { private static DateTime dateOfBirth; private static int expectedFingers; public Person(DateTime birthday) { dateOfBirth = birthday; // Noncompliant; now everyone has this birthday expectedFingers = 10; // Noncompliant } }Compliant solutionpublic class Person { private DateTime dateOfBirth; private static int expectedFingers = 10; public Person(DateTime birthday) { this.dateOfBirth = birth
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Static field is being updated in an instance constructor - this affects all instances

logic.reasoning: Assigning a value to a static field in a constructor changes the value for all instances of the class. This is usually unintended and leads to unreliable runtime behavior. Static fields should be initialized inline or in a static constructor.
